STM32_Communication
  • API Reference
  • View page source

API Reference

STM32 Class

Methods

STM32.__init__(port)[source]

Initialize serial connection to STM32 device.

Parameters:

port – Serial port name (e.g., ‘COM3’ on Windows, ‘/dev/ttyUSB0’ on Linux)

Raises:

serial.SerialException – If the port cannot be opened

STM32.send_command(command)[source]

Send a text command to the STM32 device.

Commands are sent as UTF-8 encoded strings with a newline terminator.

Parameters:

command – Command string to send (without newline)

STM32.receive_line() → bytes[source]

Read a single line from the serial port.

Returns:

Bytes object containing the line (including newline if present)

STM32.receive_data(size: int) → bytes[source]

Read exactly size bytes from the serial port.

Parameters:

size – Number of bytes to read

Returns:

Bytes object containing the received data

STM32.receive_file(file: str) → bytes[source]

Request and receive a file from the STM32 device.

Sends a GET command and parses the response metadata to determine the file size, then reads the raw file data.

Parameters:

file – Name of the file to request

Returns:

Raw bytes of the file (including packet framing)

Raises:

ValueError – If the response metadata format is unexpected

static STM32.unstuff(data: bytes) → bytes[source]

Remove byte stuffing from raw data.

Decodes escaped sequences:

  • 0xFE 0x00 → 0xFE

  • 0xFE 0x01 → 0xFF

  • 0xFE 0xE4 → 0x1A (Ctrl+Z)

Parameters:

data – Stuffed bytes to decode

Returns:

Unstuffed bytes

static STM32.crc16_update(crc, data)[source]

Update CRC with one byte.

Internal method used by crc16_compute. Implements the CRC-16/Modbus algorithm.

Parameters:

  • crc – Current CRC value (0-0xFFFF)

  • data – Byte to process (0-0xFF)

Returns:

Updated CRC value (0-0xFFFF)

static STM32.crc16_compute(data) → int[source]

Compute CRC-16/Modbus over the given data.

Algorithm:

  • Polynomial: 0xA001

  • Initial value: 0xFFFF

  • Byte order: Little-endian

Parameters:

data – Bytes to compute CRC over

Returns:

16-bit CRC value (0-0xFFFF)

static STM32.parse_packet(data: bytes) → bytes | None[source]

Parse a single packet from raw data.

Extracts timestamp, chunks, and validates CRC. The packet must start with sync marker 0xFFFF.

Packet structure (after unstuffing):

  • Timestamp: uint32_t (4 bytes, little-endian)

  • Packet Size: uint16_t (2 bytes, little-endian) = unstuffed size - 2

  • Chunks: Variable (see parse_chunks)

  • CRC16: uint16_t (2 bytes, little-endian)

Parameters:

data – Raw bytes starting with sync marker

Returns:

Dictionary with keys ‘timestamp’ (int) and ‘chunks’ (dict of chunk_id -> samples) Returns None if packet is invalid or CRC mismatch

static STM32.parse_data(data)[source]

Parse multiple concatenated packets from a data stream.

Scans for sync markers (0xFFFF) and extracts packets sequentially.

Parameters:

data – Raw bytes containing one or more packets

Returns:

List of parsed packet dictionaries (None for invalid packets)

Constants

STM32.CRC16_POLY: int = 0xA001

CRC-16/Modbus polynomial in reflected form.
